additional_rules.name: panic-in-handler
additional_rules.message: panic() in handler — return errors instead; only Recovery middleware should catch panics
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace panic() with an error return. Use c.AbortWithStatusJSON() to send an error response, or attach to c.Error() and let centralized error middleware respond.

additional_rules.name: global-state-package-var
additional_rules.message: Package-level variable — use dependency injection via constructors instead of global state
additional_rules.fix_hint: Move the variable into a struct and inject it via the constructor (e.g., NewUserHandler(...)). Package-level vars for singletons are acceptable only in main().

additional_rules.name: ignored-error-return
additional_rules.message: Potential ignored error return — ensure all error return values are checked
additional_rules.fix_hint: Check the error return value. If the error is intentionally ignored, use _ = to make it explicit.
additional_rules._note: Structural check — layer-2 linter (golangci-lint errcheck) provides accurate detection. This rule documents the convention.

additional_rules.name: dto-struct-in-handler
additional_rules.message: Request/Response struct defined in handler file — move to model/ or a dedicated dto/ package
additional_rules.fix_hint: Extract the struct to internal/model/ (domain types) or internal/handler/dto/ (request/response types). Handler structs (e.g., UserHandler) are fine in handler files.

additional_rules.name: sql-query-in-handler
additional_rules.message: SQL query in handler file — database access belongs in repository layer
additional_rules.fix_hint: Move the SQL query to a repository method in internal/repository/.
